enforced.oracle: After toggling RNEXT between '=' and the explicit RNAME value (when RNEXT==RNAME), the parsed record should be semantically equivalent. The spec explicitly allows '=' as a synonym for 'same as RNAME'.

evidence.quote: This field is set as `*' when the information is unavailable, and set as `=' if RNEXT is identical RNAME.

enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) — SEQ field letter case is explicitly unconstrained by the spec ('no assumptions can be made on the letter cases'); uppercasing the SEQ field must produce an identical parsed record

evidence.quote: No assumptions can be made on the letter cases.

enforced.oracle: error_consensus: all SUTs must REJECT or CRASH on a record where CIGAR query-consuming ops (M,I,S,=,X) sum does not equal len(SEQ); silent acceptance or SILENT_SKIP is a conformance bug

evidence.quote: If not a `*', the length of the sequence must equal the sum of lengths of MIS=X operations in CIGAR.

enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) after canonical normalization; BAM is the binary equivalent of SAM per SAMv1 §4, so round-tripping through the BAM codec must preserve all information content

evidence.quote: SAM stands for Sequence Alignment/Map format. It is a TAB-delimited text format consisting of a header section, which is optional, and an alignment section.

enforced.mr_name: SEQ case normalization preserves query method results
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method

evidence.quote: As with alignment optional fields (see Section~sec:alnaux), you can freely add new tags for further data fields.

enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method

evidence.quote: SAM stands for Sequence Alignment/Map format. It is a TAB-delimited text format consisting of a header section, which is optional, and an alignment section.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM optional tag field order permutation on alignment records
quarantine.oracle: After permuting the order of optional TAG:TYPE:VALUE fields on each alignment line, the parsed record must be semantically identical. The canonical normalizer sorts optional tags, so the oracle passes when parse(x) == parse(T(x)) after normalization.

evidence.quote: Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.
Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM @HD header line subtag field order permutation
quarantine.oracle: After shuffling the TAG:VALUE pairs within the @HD header line, the parsed header must be semantically identical. The canonical normalizer sorts subtags within each header line, so the oracle passes when parse(x) == parse(T(x)).

evidence.quote: Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.

ambiguity_flags: The spec quote about 'order not significant' is from the alignment section context; however the same principle applies to header lines per the header section description. The header section (p83) states 'Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant' but that chunk was above threshold.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM @SQ header line subtag field order permutation
quarantine.oracle: After shuffling the TAG:VALUE pairs within each @SQ header line, the parsed header must be semantically identical. The canonical normalizer sorts subtags within each header line, so the oracle passes when parse(x) == parse(T(x)).

evidence.quote: Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.

ambiguity_flags: The spec quote about 'order not significant' is from the alignment section context; however the same principle applies to header lines per the header section description.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM @RG header line subtag field order permutation
quarantine.oracle: After shuffling the TAG:VALUE pairs within each @RG header line, the parsed header must be semantically identical. The canonical normalizer sorts subtags within each header line, so the oracle passes when parse(x) == parse(T(x)).

evidence.quote: Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.

ambiguity_flags: The spec quote about 'order not significant' is from the alignment section context; however the same principle applies to header lines per the header section description.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM @PG header line subtag field order permutation
quarantine.oracle: After shuffling the TAG:VALUE pairs within each @PG header line, the parsed header must be semantically identical. The canonical normalizer sorts subtags within each header line, so the oracle passes when parse(x) == parse(T(x)).

evidence.quote: Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.

ambiguity_flags: The spec quote about 'order not significant' is from the alignment section context; however the same principle applies to header lines per the header section description.

quarantine.oracle: After shuffling the order of @CO comment lines in the header, the parsed file must be semantically identical. @CO lines are free-text comments with no ordering semantics per SAMv1 §1.3.

evidence.quote: Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.

ambiguity_flags: The spec defines @CO as free-text comments (matching /94@CO92t.*/) but does not explicitly state they have no ordering semantics. However, since @CO lines lack TAG:VALUE structure and are explicitly excluded from the 'order not significant' rule that applies to other header lines, their ordering is implicitly unconstrained.

quarantine.oracle: After reordering @SQ header lines, the parsed file must be semantically identical. The order of @SQ lines defines the coordinate sort order, but the biological semantics of the alignments themselves are unchanged.

evidence.quote: Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.

ambiguity_flags: The @SQ line order defines the coordinate sort key ordering per @HD SO:coordinate. While the biological data is unchanged, the coordinate sort order changes. This MR tests that parsers accept any @SQ order without error, even if the SO tag claims coordinate sort.

quarantine.mr_name: Splitting or merging adjacent identical CIGAR ops preserves semantics
quarantine.oracle: After splitting a CIGAR op into two identical ops (e.g., 10M -> 4M6M) or merging adjacent identical ops (4M6M -> 10M), the total query-consumed length is preserved and the alignment semantics are unchanged.

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p134
evidence.quote: Adjacent CIGAR operations should be different.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: The spec says 'should' (recommended practice, not mandatory), and the transform preserves total consumed length which is the critical semantic property.

quarantine.mr_name: Bump HD VN minor version preserves semantics
quarantine.oracle: After toggling the @HD VN field between SAM versions 1.5 and 1.6, the parsed file should be semantically equivalent since 1.5->1.6 only added clarifications without breaking backward compatibility.

evidence.quote: VN* & Format version. Accepted format: /94[0-9]+92.[0-9]+\$/.

ambiguity_flags: The spec defines the VN format but does not explicitly state that minor version bumps are backward-compatible; this is inferred from semantic versioning conventions.

quarantine.mr_name: CIGAR zero-length op removal normalization invariance
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) — zero-length CIGAR ops carry no alignment information; stripping them and merging newly-adjacent same-type ops is a canonical normalization that must produce identical parsed alignment coordinates

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p134
evidence.quote: * Adjacent CIGAR operations should be different.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: The spec says 'should' (advisory) not 'must'; zero-length ops are not explicitly forbidden, only discouraged via the adjacent-ops rule

quarantine.mr_name: CIGAR hard/soft clipping equivalence normalization invariance
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) — converting between hard-clipping (H) and soft-clipping (S) in CIGAR with synchronized SEQ/QUAL padding/trimming is a clipping-representation equivalence; the mapped portion of the alignment must be identical after parsing

evidence.quote: H can only be present as the first and/or last operation. S may only have H operations between them and the ends of the CIGAR string.

ambiguity_flags: H→S conversion requires padding SEQ with dummy bases which changes the raw SEQ string; parsers that expose raw SEQ rather than the aligned portion may show differences

quarantine.mr_name: Flag bit 0x4 set on mapped record rejection
quarantine.oracle: error_consensus: all SUTs must REJECT or CRASH when FLAG 0x4 (unmapped) is set on a record with RNAME != '*' and POS > 0; silent acceptance is a conformance bug

evidence.quote: Bit 0x4 is the only reliable place to tell whether the read is unmapped. If 0x4 is set, no assumptions can be made about RNAME, POS, CIGAR, MAPQ, and bits 0x2, 0x100, and 0x800.

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p137
evidence.quote: If all segments in a template are unmapped, their RNAME should be set as `*' and POS as 0.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: The spec says 'no assumptions can be made' rather than explicitly forbidding the combination, but the recommended practice and Picard validation rules treat this as an error

quarantine.mr_name: Illegal optional tag type character rejection
quarantine.oracle: error_consensus: all SUTs must REJECT or CRASH when an optional tag's TYPE character is not one of A,i,f,Z,H,B; silent acceptance is a conformance bug

evidence.quote: All optional fields follow the TAG:TYPE:VALUE format where TAG is a two-character string that matches /[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9]/.

quarantine.oracle: error_consensus: all SUTs must REJECT or CRASH when two reads of a paired template have same-signed TLEN values (both positive or both negative); silent acceptance is a conformance bug

evidence.quote: The TLEN field is positive for the leftmost segment of the template, negative for the rightmost, and the sign for any middle segment is undefined. If segments cover the same coordinates then the choice of which is leftmost and rightmost is arbitrary, but the two ends must still have differing signs.

ambiguity_flags: The spec allows undefined sign for middle segments in templates with >2 segments, but the two ends must have differing signs

quarantine.mr_name: POS shift with SQ LN bound check preserves coordinate consistency
quarantine.oracle: After shifting POS by +shift and increasing @SQ LN by +shift, for every mapped alignment record, POS + sum_of_ref_consuming_CIGAR_ops(M,=,X,D,N) <= LN. The shift preserves the relative coordinate system: all valid alignments remain within bounds.

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p138
evidence.quote: If POS plus the sum of lengths of M=XDN operations in CIGAR exceeds the length specified in the LN field of the @SQ header line (if exists) with an SN equal to RNAME, the alignment should be unmapped, unless the reference sequence is circular (see below).
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: ADVISORY severity — 'should be unmapped' is a recommendation, not a hard requirement; circular references are an exception

quarantine.mr_name: Unmapped record MAPQ normalization preserves coordinate semantics
quarantine.oracle: For records with FLAG bit 0x4 set, MAPQ is normalized to 255 (the spec-defined sentinel for 'not available'). RNAME, POS, and CIGAR are left untouched per SAMv1 §1.4 which states 'no assumptions can be made' about these fields for unmapped reads. The coordinate system is preserved as-is.

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p138
evidence.quote: If POS plus the sum of lengths of M=XDN operations in CIGAR exceeds the length specified in the LN field of the @SQ header line (if exists) with an SN equal to RNAME, the alignment should be unmapped, unless the reference sequence is circular (see below).
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: ADVISORY severity — the spec does not mandate MAPQ=255 for unmapped reads; this is a Picard/htsjdk convention

quarantine.mr_name: CIGAR zero-length op removal preserves query method results
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method

evidence.quote: SAM stands for Sequence Alignment/Map format. It is a TAB-delimited text format consisting of a header section, which is optional, and an alignment section.

ambiguity_flags: SAM Recommended Practice (p134) says 'adjacent CIGAR operations should be different' but does not mandate zero-length op removal
